# UC-17.6.11 · Akamai Guardicore Agent Update Compliance and Version Drift (`guardicore:agent`)

> **Criticality:** High &middot; **Difficulty:** Intermediate &middot; **Pillar:** Security &middot; **Type:** Configuration, Compliance &middot; **Status:** Verified

*We use network security tools to watch who accesses what, help prevent data leaks, enforce network isolation, and secure cloud and remote access.*

---

## Description

Version skew hints at broken deployment pipelines or dormant VMs.

## Value

Security maintains a single supported agent baseline.

## Implementation

Install the add-on, configure API polling intervals appropriate to API limits, and map assets to CMDB for enrichment lookups.

## SPL

```spl
index=microseg sourcetype="guardicore:agent" earliest=-4h
| stats values(agent_version) as versions dc(hostname) as hosts by datacenter
| eval unique=mvsort(versions)
| where mvcount(unique)>1
| sort -hosts
```

## CIM SPL

```spl
| tstats `summariesonly` count
  from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic
  by All_Traffic.user, All_Traffic.action, All_Traffic.src, All_Traffic.dest span=1h
| sort - count
```

## Visualization

Table (datacenter, versions), pie.

## Known False Positives

Microseg blocks from new app deployments, missing exception rules, or planned segmentation rollouts.

## References

- [Splunkbase — Akamai Guardicore Add-on for Splunk](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/7426)
- [Akamai Guardicore documentation](https://www.akamai.com/products/guardicore)
